props.title: Everything John sent. Everything you decided.
props.subtitle: 21 files, 5 versions, 1 tagline stack awaiting go/no-go.
props.meta: Prepared 2026-04-22 · Source: John (+18478631269) iMessage · Workstream: herenow-content

props.content: John has run through two full reframes and a v2.1 addition in six days. The Apache-core, Year-2-hosted-cloud, signal-based launch posture you set on April 17 is still locked. Three new tagline lines want your yes/no before they propagate to README and leviathan.dev.

props.title: The numbers that matter right now

rows.purpose: July Dev.to byline — needs JP review

props.label: POSITIONING STACK v2.1
props.title: Awaiting your line-by-line yes/no
props.subtitle: 13 lines total. 7 JP-endorsed (untouched). 3 sharpened. 1 filled TBD. 3 net-new differentiation lines.

props.content: John's reasoning for v2.1: the v2 stack was feature-descriptive and under-used five differentiation threads — middle-harness posture, model-agnosticism, no single point of failure, user-owned second brain, graph compounds with use. v2.1 adds three lines to carry those threads without touching any of your endorsed anchors. **The anchor metaphor is CSS-of-Agents — portable, model-agnostic, auditable via view-source. Every line threads into that frame.**

rows.line: Leviathan is the CSS of agents.
One runtime. Any model. Every surface. Your graph.
rows.change: SHARPENED (was 'One runtime. Every agent. Every surface.')

rows.line: Swap the model. Keep the agent. Own the logs.
Write it once. Run it anywhere. Own everything.
Your agents. Your graph. Your keys. Your second brain — portable.
No single provider. No single point of failure. Use any model per task.
Every action leased. Every lease logged. Every log replayable.
Trust is earned. Permissions expire. Audit trail is forever.
Your agent's long-term memory. Your auditor's dream. Your graph compounds with use.
rows.change: CODA TIGHTENED (was 'patterns strengthen, rest decay — nothing is ever lost')

rows.line: Workflows portable. Graph portable. Proof portable.
Run agents for free. Govern agents for money.
The open runtime every agent platform should have built on.
CDO is the TCP of thinking.

props.label: WEDGE PORTFOLIO
props.title: Three wedges, one frame
props.subtitle: JP hard-rejected single-wedge strategy on Apr 17. Replay, AgentLease, and Article 12 alignment are capabilities threaded through all three — not standalone wedges.

props.title: 🏗 PRIMARY · Agent Compiler
props.content: "Leviathan is the CSS of agents." FlowMind's 6-target compiler (smartdown + system prompt + openprose + hooks + schedule + prompt_gen) + Poly's multi-surface output (CLI + MCP + HTTP + gRPC) delivers the CSS metaphor: one declarative spec, every surface. `leviathan migrate` ships day one to counter MS Agent Framework's 75K-star distribution. **Anchor: your Apr 17 casual phrase 'agent compiler' + your endorsed CSS metaphor.**

props.title: 🖥 SECONDARY · Surfaces
props.content: "Agents don't chat. They act." AgentPing-centered. Browser buttons, voice commands, VR gestures, Stream Deck keys, mobile notifications. Runs as a **parallel wedge**, not a subordinate. Sub-hero v2.1 now anchors this with both multi-surface and any-model claims.

props.title: 🔗 TERTIARY · Graph
props.content: "Your agent's long-term memory. Your auditor's dream." Append-only, bi-temporal, never overwrites. Graph plane name **retained**. Carries regulatory-alignment story: EU AI Act Article 12, DORA 9/17/25, NIST AI RMF, Texas TRAIGA affirmative defense. v2.1 coda 'compounds with use' adds ownership-over-time.

props.label: LAUNCH FRAMEWORK
props.title: Five-green-lights signal gate
props.subtitle: Launch fires when 4 of 5 signals turn green simultaneously, OR the October 31 2026 hard ceiling fires the fallback. No extensions.

props.title: Signal 1 · Dogfood green
props.content: JP has used Leviathan daily for at least 14 consecutive days on real work with zero catastrophic failures. **John's private dogfood hit same bar (stays private, never public).** FlowMind compile cycle clean end-to-end. Event-bus JSONL replay reproduces any session byte-for-byte. **v4 note:** John's commitment on Apr 20 call to install Leviathan this week is the unblocking action for this signal.

props.title: Signal 2 · Design-partner green
props.content: Three quiet design partners running Leviathan in real workflows. Each has logged at least one week of JSONL session data without showstopper bugs. **At least one partner is a working developer outside JP and John's immediate network.** Target: 5 approached, 3 accepting by mid-June.

props.title: Signal 3 · Unsolicited-inbound green
props.content: Five or more unsolicited inquiries per month for two consecutive months. 'Unsolicited' means people asking about Leviathan because they heard about it, not because JP posted and they responded. GitHub issues from unknown accounts count. Stars without engagement don't count.

props.title: Signal 4 · Narrative green
props.content: 1-sentence pitch, 1-paragraph pitch, and 3-demo storyboard tested on at least 10 developers not already in the JP/John thread. **7 or more of 10 can describe Leviathan back correctly without prompting.** v4 tests CSS-for-Agents framing specifically. If fewer than 7/10 land with CSS framing, fall back to bare 'one spec, six surfaces' lead copy.

props.title: Signal 5 · Competitive-forcing green
props.content: Either a competitor threatens to take the position (MAF ships multi-surface codegen, Encore pivots to agents, new YC S26 entrant targets same wedge) OR a supporting-framing narrative wave creates a rare window (EU AI Act high-risk-clause activation Aug 2, 2026). **Signal 5 alone can accelerate launch** if Signals 1-4 are within a week of green.

props.label: RISK REGISTER v4
props.title: Top 10 of 20 tracked risks
props.subtitle: Escalations and new additions vs v3 noted inline.

rows.risk: Cloudflare Agents stack becomes default platform in 90 days (escalated v4)
rows.mitigation: Weekly blog monitoring. If consolidation announced May 1-20, fire Signal 5 + accelerate.

rows.risk: Anthropic Claude Managed Agents dominates 'managed' category (NEW v4)
rows.mitigation: Remove 'managed' from all public copy. Use sovereign/self-hosted/protocol-set/BYO-infra.

rows.risk: Mastra Memory Gateway expands into replay territory (NEW v4)
rows.mitigation: Watch first Mastra technical blog. If replay-adjacent, reframe Framing B.
Monthly devblog check. If MAF adds multi-target compile, 'Replay and remember' becomes lead.
USPTO file Month 2, not Month 6. Backup names pre-vetted. **JP: txt convo only, advisory.**
John's dogfood = partial mitigation. Full mitigation: second committer pre-launch.

rows.risk: JP reopens wedge or licensing debate
rows.mitigation: This doc + Wedge v4 state explicitly both settled. Reopening = new strategy doc.

rows.risk: Enterprise posture returns to conversation (escalated v4)
rows.mitigation: JP rejected twice (Apr 17 + Apr 20). Third rejection would be severe. Do not revisit.

rows.risk: 'CSS for Agents' framing fails Signal 4 testing (NEW v4)
rows.mitigation: If <7/10 devs describe Leviathan back correctly with CSS framing, fall back to bare 'one spec, six surfaces' lead.

rows.risk: Mesh / agent.gg / faba.gg pull toward Year-2 prematurely (NEW v4)
rows.mitigation: Mesh is parked as Year-2 arc. Framing K ('Runtime first, mesh next') ships only after compiler wedge lands.

props.label: VOCABULARY (v4)
props.title: Banned words · what to say instead
props.subtitle: Anthropic owns 'managed' at the agent layer. Nate Jones's Apr 17 video trained builder audience to read 'managed' as 'walled garden'.

props.title: ❌ BANNED in public copy
props.content:
**managed**
**managed AI infrastructure**
**managed platform**
**framework**
**discipline**
**workflow discipline**
**methodology**
**phases**
**mandatory**
**declarative YAML agent definition** (Microsoft owns this phrasing)
**The Vercel for AI agents** (Vercel claimed Apr 13)
**Kubernetes of agents**
**operating system for agents**

props.title: ✅ REPLACE WITH
props.content:
**sovereign agent runtime**
**self-hosted agent gateway**
**protocol set and runtime**
**bring-your-own-infrastructure runtime**

*Your D21 (Apr 20): 'Sovereign data portability, OSS/local-first, simple file-based graph starting point, air-gap capable with local LLM. Everything above that = upgrade.' — this is deeper than the vocab swap. Sovereignty is architectural, not branding.*

props.title: 🎤 AI Engineer World's Fair
props.content:
**Dates:** June 29 – July 2, 2026, San Francisco
**Deadline:** Rolling
**Size:** 6,000+ attendees — largest technical AI conference
**Recommendation:** Full MD + PDF recs in strategy folder. Submit ASAP; demo slot is ideal.
**Travel budget:** $2K ceiling (original Apr 17 ask)

props.title: 🐙 GitHub Universe · URGENT
props.content:
**Dates:** October 28-29, 2026, San Francisco
**Deadline:** May 1 2026 — **9 DAYS OUT**
**Talk hook:** 'Why MCP Servers Die and How We Fixed It' (from 04-17 PDF) — recommendation doc has updated framing
**Leverage:** Use weeks 3-8 deployment data (if exists) as empirical backbone
**Action:** Read `Leviathan_GitHubUniverse_CFP_Recommendation.md` (9 KB)

props.label: JOHN'S VOICE
props.title: Non-strategy signals worth reading
props.subtitle: Not every John message is a strategy doc. Some are pushback, some are nudges.

props.content:
What if we get it to blow up don't you want them to pay you for it? Like they did Peter??

— *John · 2026-04-20 14:03 — monetization nudge (who is Peter?)*

I don't understand sometime seems like you're just sending them all your work to copy some engineer who receives it just be building it on the side or customer service rep

— *John · 2026-04-20 14:03 — skeptical of open-sharing pattern*

Have you been in taking the transcript of Nate's videos everyday he is so on point I'm listening to one from 5 days ago on AI agents

— *John · 2026-04-20 15:14 — Nate B. Jones (@NateBJones) is a third-party YouTuber, not John's last name*

rows.decision: master-launch becomes new .lev/campaigns/ hub attached to herenow-content as launch-umbrella
Prior-art must precede bead filing on inbound advisor claims — 5th claim audit caught false 'all private:true'
When stop-hook mandates handoff, workstream YAML remains durable; handoff is projection pointer only
Sovereign data portability + OSS/local-first is architectural primitive, not vocab swap. Simple file-based graph = starting point. Air-gap + local LLM = baseline. Everything above that = upgrade.
Armada Leviathan $131M trademark was txt conversation only, not action item. USPTO Month-2 filing stays advisory per JP.
No vocabulary-sweep bead needed. Existing copy is clean; 'managed' is a go-forward constraint on new copy only.
bead lev-j9nwx CLOSED as superseded by v4. bead lev-byhkd is new P0 launch-tracker; launches fire on 5-signal gate not calendar.

props.label: OPEN ASKS TO JP
props.title: What needs your yes/no right now
props.subtitle: Ordered by urgency.

props.title: 1 · Approve v2.1 tagline stack (7 lines)
props.content:
3 new + 3 sharpened + 1 filled TBD. Unblocks: README + leviathan.dev landing copy, Phase 2 wedge files, Signal 4 narrative testing in June.

**Fastest path:** reply 'all approved' in Discord/iMessage to John.

**Alternative:** line-by-line yes/no via `bd update lev-byhkd --notes`.

props.title: 2 · GitHub Universe CFP (9 days)
props.content: May 1 deadline. Recommendation doc at `strategy/Leviathan_GitHubUniverse_CFP_Recommendation.md` (9 KB). JP + talk title decision needed.

props.title: 3 · Publish JSONL event schema as standalone spec
props.content: v2.1 calls this the **highest-leverage artifact** — anchors Lifecycle line (replay) + Graph line (audit trail) + Portable-workflows line (proof portable) + EU AI Act Article 12 story simultaneously. Quadruple leverage.

props.title: 4 · env.process leak sweep (P0 security)
props.content: 3/4 P0 security items fixed (staged, uncommitted). Env-leak sweep across 14+ sites in core/harness/*, core/poly/*, core/flowmind/router.policy.ts remains. Recommend 3 sub-beads: lev-jy2cm.1 (harness), .2 (poly), .3 (flowmind).

props.title: 5 · Commit 3 staged security fixes
props.content: `APIKeyVault.js` (createCipheriv), `validation-gate-executor.ts` (Function guard), `drift-cache.ts` (spawnSync). +35/-38 LOC, tsc clean. Clean audit trail for lev-jy2cm.

props.label: IF THIS IS RIGHT
props.title: The fastest path forward
props.subtitle: Strategy is settled. These are the actions that convert it into artifacts.

props.content: You have 9 days to GitHub Universe. 14 days of JP dogfood starts whenever you decide it starts. Everything else is cadence.

**Tonight:** reply to John with the v2.1 tagline verdict (all-approved is fine — nothing you endorsed has changed).

**This week:** pick the GitHub Universe talk angle. Start Signal 1 dogfood counter.

**Next week:** quality-floor month starts (vulnerability fixes, TypeScript 52/57, LICENSE files, trademark prep if you want it). Publish JSONL event schema as standalone spec.

**Don't touch:** the 7 JP-endorsed tagline lines, the Apache-core / Year-2-hosted-cloud licensing, the signal-based launch posture, the October 31 hard ceiling. These are settled and John has documented them as settled. Re-opening any of them triggers another strategy doc cycle.
